weeklyFocus.headline: Galaxy S26 Launch Week + 3rd Line Free
weeklyFocus.context: Samsung Galaxy S26 series launched March 11. iPhone 17e also just dropped. Push BTS add-ons on every support call — Galaxy Watch8 is FREE with wearable line.

currentPromos.name: Galaxy S26 Ultra FREE
currentPromos.details: Free with new line on Experience Beyond (no trade-in needed, up to $1,300 off via 24 monthly bill credits)

commonObjections.objection: I don't want to commit to 24 months of credits
commonObjections.response: The credits start on your first bill and you're never locked in — no contract. If you leave, you just pay the remaining balance on the device. Most people stay because the plan is that good.

currentPromos.name: 3rd Line Free
currentPromos.details: Add a 3rd voice line free on Experience Beyond or Experience More (via monthly bill credits). Limited time.

commonObjections.objection: What happens if I cancel a line later?
commonObjections.response: The credits are tied to keeping 3 active voice lines. If you drop below 3, the credit stops. But honestly, most families find they need the lines anyway.

currentPromos.name: Galaxy Watch8 FREE
currentPromos.details: Free via 36 monthly bill credits with new wearable line ($5/mo line cost)

commonObjections.response: True, but think about it — for $5 a month you get a device that handles calls, texts, payments, and fitness tracking. That's less than a coffee. And the watch itself is free.

currentPromos.name: iPhone 17e FREE
currentPromos.details: Free with trade-in on qualifying $60+/month plan. Great entry-level iPhone with A19 chip.

planUpdates.change: Apple TV+ pricing changed to $3/month customer cost (T-Mobile covers $9.99 of $12.99). Was fully free through end of 2025.
planUpdates.talkingPoint: Apple TV+ is still a massive deal at $3/month — that's saving you almost $10 every month compared to paying full price.

planUpdates.change: Regulatory Programs & Telco Recovery Fee increased $0.50/line to $4.49/line/month (second increase in under a year).
planUpdates.talkingPoint: This is a regulatory fee, not a plan price change. Your plan rate is still locked under the 5-Year Price Guarantee.

competitiveIntel.intel: AT&T launched Unlimited Your Way 2.0 on March 13, 2026 — stripped ALL streaming perks from every plan. Zero Netflix, zero Max, zero Disney+.
competitiveIntel.talkingPoint: AT&T just removed every streaming perk from their plans. We include Netflix, Hulu, and Apple TV+ — that's about $30/month in value they just took away from their customers.

competitiveIntel.intel: AT&T raising legacy plan prices AGAIN in April 2026 ($10-20/month increases). At least the 4th increase in ~2 years.
competitiveIntel.talkingPoint: AT&T is raising prices again next month. With our 5-Year Price Guarantee, that doesn't happen here. Your rate is locked.

competitiveIntel.intel: Verizon settled $100M class action over hidden Administrative & Telco Recovery Charge ($3.78/line/month that grew 8x from original $0.40).
competitiveIntel.talkingPoint: Verizon literally got sued for hiding fees. We're upfront about everything — and our 5-Year Price Guarantee locks your rate.

knownIssues.issue: Some customers reporting delayed bill credits for 3rd-line-free promo (credits not appearing until 2nd billing cycle)
knownIssues.workaround: Set expectations: credits typically start on the 2nd bill. If missing after 2 cycles, escalate to care for manual credit.

knownIssues.issue: Galaxy S26 Ultra back-ordered in Phantom Black 512GB — 2-3 week wait
knownIssues.workaround: Offer Phantom Silver (in stock) or suggest 256GB. If they insist on Black 512GB, set up pre-order and manage the wait expectation.

openers: What caught your eye today? No pressure — let's just explore what's out there.
Are you checking things out for yourself or for the whole family?
Have you seen any of the deals we're running right now? There's some good stuff.
discovery: What phone are you using right now?
What carrier are you on? Just curious what you're working with.
What matters most to you — price, coverage, or the phone itself?
keyMoves: Plant the seed: mention the streaming perks and 5-Year Price Guarantee
If they mention a competitor, pull up the side-by-side comparison
Offer to save a quote so they can think it over
closingTips: No pressure close: 'Let me save this for you so you have the numbers when you're ready.'
Give them your card and mention the promo deadline
avoidMoves: Don't push hard — browsers need space
Don't mention contract terms or ETFs unless they ask

openers: Let's get you set up! What are you looking to grab today?
Perfect timing — we've got some great deals running. What do you have in mind?
Awesome! Do you already know what device you want, or want me to walk you through the options?
discovery: How many lines do you need?
Are you trading in your current device? We take them in any condition.
Do you have a plan in mind, or want me to recommend one based on what you need?
keyMoves: Move quickly — they're ready. Don't over-explain.
Lead with the best current promo for their device/plan combo
Mention Protection 360 before they ask about cases
Pitch a watch or tablet as an add-on before checkout
closingTips: Direct close: 'I can get you out of here in 20 minutes with everything set up.'
Start the trade-in process immediately to build momentum
avoidMoves: Don't slow them down with too many options
Don't skip the P360 pitch — it's easy commission

openers: Looking to upgrade or add someone to the account?
Great timing — we've got some solid upgrade deals right now. What are you working with?
Are you adding a line for someone, or upgrading your own device?
discovery: What device are you on now? We can check your trade-in value.
Are you looking at the same brand, or open to switching?
Is your plan still working for you, or do you want to look at what's new?
keyMoves: Check trade-in value immediately — show them the math
If adding a line, mention the 3rd-line-free promo
Compare their current plan to Experience Beyond/More
Pitch a connected device (watch/tablet) as a bundle add-on
closingTips: Show the side-by-side: current plan vs new plan with trade-in credits applied
If adding a line: 'With the 3rd line free, you're basically getting this for nothing.'
avoidMoves: Don't assume they want the same brand — ask
Don't skip the plan review — they might be on a retired plan paying more

openers: Let me pull that up and get it sorted for you.
No worries — I deal with order stuff all day. What's going on?
I've got you. What's the order number or what are you seeing?
discovery: When did you place the order?
What's the status showing on your end?
Did you get a confirmation email or tracking number?
keyMoves: Resolve their issue FIRST — earn trust before pivoting
After resolution: 'By the way, do you have a watch or tablet on your account?'
Mention Galaxy Watch8 FREE deal or SyncUP Tracker
If they're on an old plan, mention the upgrade opportunity
closingTips: Service-to-sales: 'All set on the order! Quick question — do you have anything protecting your car or tracking your luggage?'
If they bite: 'It's only $5/month for the line, and the device is free right now.'
avoidMoves: Don't pitch before resolving their issue
Don't make them feel like the support call was just a sales opportunity

openers: Let's figure this out. What's going on with your device?
I deal with this stuff all day — we'll get it sorted.
No stress — tech issues happen. Walk me through what you're seeing.
discovery: Have you tried restarting the device?
keyMoves: Fix the issue first — build trust and gratitude
After fix: pivot to Protection 360 — 'This is exactly what P360 covers'
Mention watch as backup connectivity: 'If your phone ever acts up, a watch with cellular means you're still connected'
Check if they have an old device that could be traded in
closingTips: Gratitude pivot: 'Glad that's working! While I have you — are you using any kind of smartwatch or tracker?'
P360 pitch: 'If you had P360, stuff like this would be covered. Plus you get $0 screen repairs and JUMP! upgrades.'
avoidMoves: Don't rush the fix to get to the sale
Don't make them feel dumb about the tech issue

openers: Let me pull up your account and see what's going on.
No problem — what do you need help with on your account?
I've got you. What's the account question?
discovery: What specifically are you seeing on your account?
Is this about billing, plan changes, or something else?
How long have you been with T-Mobile?
keyMoves: Resolve their issue first
While in the account: look for missing connected devices, old plans, missing P360
After resolution: 'I noticed you don't have any connected devices — did you know a watch line is only $5/month?'
Check if they qualify for Better Value plan (5+ years tenure)
closingTips: Account review pivot: 'While I was in your account, I noticed a couple things that could save you money or add value.'
Home Internet pitch: 'With your voice line, Home Internet is only $35/month. What are you paying your ISP?'
avoidMoves: Don't upsell if they're frustrated about a billing error — fix it first
Don't assume they want to hear about new products if they're upset
